data.title: Inventor creates leaf blower attachment for cleaning gutters, known as the MASTER BLASTER, from Columbus, Neb.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 16, 2026, an inventor from Columbus, Nebraska, shared the inspiration behind the creation of the MASTER BLASTER – a new device designed to clear gutters of leaves, twigs, and debris for better water flow. This invention was born out of the inventor's idea to improve gutter cleaning by eliminating the need for standing on a ladder. The MASTER BLASTER aims to revolutionize gutter maintenance by providing a more efficient and safer alternative.

data.title: 48 Hour Books adds new customizable options for book printing in 2026, the go-to self-publishing partner. Check out 48HrBooks.com for details.
data.description: In Twinsburg, Ohio, on February 16, 2026, 48 Hour Books, a prominent self-publishing partner specializing in rapid and top-notch book printing, has introduced fresh customization choices for the year. Those interested can explore 48 Hour Books' various book printing alternatives and resources for self-publishing by visiting their official website at 48HrBooks.com. The company has been providing these services for more than a decade now.

data.title: Rapidise, an India-based ODM, sees 10X revenue growth, expanding globally in electronics design and manufacturing.
data.description: On February 16, 2026, in San Francisco, Rapidise, an ODM company based in India, is expanding rapidly to become a globally competitive ESDM company. This growth phase includes a significant increase in engineering capability and is propelling the company into a new era of hyper-growth.

data.title: IBA discloses information on its share buyback program announced on Feb 2, 2026 as per Belgian regulations.
data.description: Louvain-la-Neuve, Belgium, February 16, 2026, at 6 PM CET: Ion Beam Applications SA ("IBA") is complying with article 8:4 of the Royal Decree dated April 29, 2019, which implements the Belgian Code on Companies and Associations. This disclosure pertains to IBA's share buyback initiative launched on February 2, 2026.

data.title: Article questions fairness of MCFT, SLAB, WBS, and TWO deals for shareholders due to insider benefits and potential limitations on competing offers. Shareholders urged to seek guidance.
data.description: Those with inside connections might gain significant financial advantages that regular shareholders do not have access to. The suggested deals may have conditions that restrict better competing bids. Shareholders are urged to reach out to the company to talk about their rights and choices without incurring any expenses.

data.title: Global MEMS packaging market to reach $85.6B by 2030 with 10.1% CAGR, growing from $48.08B in 2024. AI sensors drive growth.
data.description: In Bangalore, India, as of February 16, 2026, the market size of MEMS Packaging is a significant factor to consider. The global market for MEMS Packaging is estimated to increase from USD 48,080 million in 2024 to USD 85,640 million by 2030. This growth reflects a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 10.1% throughout the forecast period. It is crucial to track this growth and stay updated on market trends to maximize potential opportunities in this sector.

data.title: BNB holders gained 177% returns in 15 months via stacking rewards. They earned $553 per token from Jan '24 to Mar '25, averaging 11.8% monthly.
data.description: According to data from Binance, individuals holding BNB and engaging in Launchpool and airdrop activities saw a profit of $553 per token between January 2024 and March 2025, resulting in a monthly average return of 11.8%.

data.title: InventHelp inventor from Lake Havasu City creates RICK'S TACKLE protective container for fishing lures.
data.description: An inventor from Lake Havasu City, Arizona, shared his motivation behind creating the RICK'S TACKLE on February 16, 2026. The creative solution aimed to secure and stabilize painted lures and hard baits, ultimately preventing tangling and damage. This innovative design was born out of a desire to protect and preserve expensive fishing equipment.

data.title: KPN bought 1,000,000 shares at €4.58 each for €4.6m as part of €250m buyback plan, showing commitment to shareholders.
data.description: In the week between February 9 and February 13 of 2026, KPN bought back 1,000,000 of its ordinary shares at an average price of €4.58 per share, totaling €4.6 million. These transactions are included in the company's €250 million share buyback initiative launched on January 29, 2026, demonstrating KPN's dedication to consistently providing additional value to its investors through returning capital.
